en.title: Best Espresso Machines of 2026: Home Machines Ranked for Shot Quality, Durability and Value
en.description: The home espresso machines worth buying in 2026, ranked on shot quality and temperature stability, steam power, ease of use, build quality, and value across every budget.

zh-tw.title: 2026 最佳義式咖啡機排行榜：萃取品質、耐用度與性價比實測推薦
zh-tw.description: 2026 年值得買的家用義式咖啡機，從萃取品質與溫控穩定度、蒸汽奶泡、上手難度、做工耐用度到性價比，各價格帶完整排名。

en.description: An all-in-one machine with a built-in conical burr grinder and an assisted tamping lever that applies 22 pounds of force on a single pull, plus an auto-dose correction system that tells you to add or remove grounds before you brew.

zh-tw.description: 一台把磨豆機直接內建進去的全能機，扳一次輔助填壓桿就給你 22 磅壓力，還會自動判斷粉量多寡叫你加粉或減粉再萃取。

en.description: A fully automatic bean-to-cup machine with 18 pre-programmed recipes, a full-colour touch display, swappable bean hoppers for switching between two coffees, and a LatteCrema milk system with three foam densities.

zh-tw.description: 一鍵到底的全自動機，內建 18 種預設配方，全彩觸控螢幕操作，豆槽可以整個抽換讓你在兩種豆子之間切換，LatteCrema 奶泡系統有三種綿密度可選。

en.description: A 7.5-inch-wide thermojet machine that reaches brew temperature in three seconds and pairs it with an automatic steam wand that hits your chosen milk temperature and foam level, then shuts off on its own.

zh-tw.description: 機身只有 19 公分寬，thermojet 加熱三秒就到萃取溫度，搭配全自動蒸汽管，設定好牛奶溫度跟奶泡厚度它會自己打完自己停。

en.description: An Italian-built single boiler with a 58 mm commercial portafilter, a three-way solenoid valve, a 9-bar OPV set at the factory, and stainless steel internals that the modding community has been rebuilding and upgrading for decades.

zh-tw.description: 義大利製單鍋爐機，配 58mm 商用把手、三通電磁閥、原廠就調好 9 bar 的洩壓閥，內部不鏽鋼件多，改機社群玩了幾十年零件都還找得到。

en.description: A German-engineered compact single boiler with a factory PID, an OLED display, ring brew group, programmable on/off timer, optional pre-infusion, and dual illuminated brew buttons with built-in shot timers.

zh-tw.description: 德國設計的小型單鍋爐機，原廠就有 PID 溫控跟 OLED 螢幕，環形沖煮頭，可以設定自動開關機時間跟預浸泡，兩顆發光萃取鍵各自內建計時。

en.description: A dual boiler in a compact commercial-grade steel body, with independent PID control over brew and steam, a rotating steam wand carried over from Rancilio's café machines, and parts availability that keeps these running for fifteen years.

zh-tw.description: 雙鍋爐塞進商用等級的鋼製小機身，萃取跟蒸汽各自獨立 PID 控溫，蒸汽管直接沿用 Rancilio 商用機的可旋轉設計，零件供應好到這台能用十五年。

en.description: A dual boiler E61 machine with a manual flow-control paddle that lets you shape pressure live during the shot, plumbable or tank-fed, with LCC control over both boilers and a walnut-trimmed stainless body.

zh-tw.description: 雙鍋爐 E61 機種，最大賣點是手動流量控制撥桿，萃取過程中可以即時調整壓力曲線，可接水管也可用水箱，兩顆鍋爐都能用 LCC 控制，機身不鏽鋼配胡桃木飾板。

en.description: A true dual boiler with PID temperature control, a heated 58 mm group head, programmable pre-infusion pressure and duration, and a shot clock, at roughly half what Italian dual boilers cost.

zh-tw.description: 貨真價實的雙鍋爐配 PID 溫控，58mm 沖煮頭有獨立加熱，預浸泡的壓力跟時間都能自己設定，還有萃取計時，價格大概只要義大利雙鍋爐機的一半。

en.description: A bean-to-cup automatic whose LatteGo milk carafe has just two dishwasher-safe parts and no tubes at all, paired with a ceramic burr grinder with 12 settings and an AquaClean filter rated for 5,000 cups before descaling.

zh-tw.description: 全自動豆到杯機，LatteGo 奶泡壺整組只有兩個零件而且沒有任何管路，可以直接丟洗碗機，陶瓷磨盤有 12 段研磨度，AquaClean 濾芯可以撐到 5,000 杯才需要除垢。

en.description: A 15-bar pump machine with a stainless steel boiler and a manual milk frother that pulls genuine pressurized espresso for under $150, in a footprint small enough for a dorm counter.

zh-tw.description: 15 bar 幫浦配不鏽鋼鍋爐，加一支手動奶泡管，用一張小朋友出頭的價格就能做出真正有壓力萃取的濃縮，機身小到宿舍書桌都放得下。

references.title: The best espresso machines in 2026, tried and tested
The best budget espresso machines in 2026
Gaggia Classic E24 Evo Pro review

en.commentary:
The Breville Barista Express Impress takes the top spot in 2026 because it solves the one problem that ends most home espresso journeys: the grind. Every machine below $1,500 can pull a decent shot once the dose and tamp are right, and getting those right by hand is where beginners quit. The Impress grinds, doses, tells you when you are 2 grams over, and tamps at 22 pounds with one lever pull. I have watched people go from cafe-only to pulling repeatable shots inside a week on this machine, and that is worth more than the marginal temperature stability a prosumer machine buys you.

Breville's Dual Boiler at second is the enthusiast pick that stays sane about money. A true dual boiler with PID and programmable pre-infusion for around $1,700 undercuts the Italian equivalents by a full thousand dollars, and the shot quality gap is small enough that most drinkers will never taste it.

The Gaggia Classic Evo Pro at third earns its place on a fifteen-year horizon. A 58 mm commercial portafilter, a three-way solenoid, and a parts supply that has kept these alive since the nineties mean the $499 you spend today is the last espresso machine purchase you need to make. Add a $150 PID kit later and it competes with machines four times its price.

The Lelit Bianca V3 and Rancilio Silvia Pro X sit fifth and sixth on merit rather than performance. Both pull better espresso than anything above them. Both ask for a $400 grinder, a scale, a distribution tool, and a real learning commitment before they show you what they can do. Rank them by what they deliver on day one and they land here. Rank them by what they deliver in year three and they are first and second.

The De'Longhi Stilosa at tenth still deserves a place on this list. For $99 it answers the only question that matters before you spend $700: do you actually want to make espresso every morning, or do you want to want to?

highlights.title: The grinder is the machine, and Breville figured that out first
highlights.body: Espresso is 90% grind consistency and dose accuracy. A $3,000 Lelit fed by a $60 blade grinder makes worse coffee than a $650 Impress with its built-in conical burr, and that is not a close call. The Impress goes further with dose correction, showing you a fill-level indicator before you tamp so you know whether to add or remove grounds. That single feature removes the most common beginner failure, which is a wildly inconsistent dose producing gushers one morning and chokes the next. If you are buying a separate grinder anyway, budget $400 minimum for it, and at that point the all-in-one math changes completely.

highlights.title: Buy the Gaggia Classic Evo Pro if you want to still own it in 2041
highlights.body: This is the only machine on the list with a genuine multi-decade track record. Gaggia has kept the Classic platform in production since 1991, and the parts ecosystem reflects it: gaskets, solenoids, boilers, OPV springs, and full PID retrofit kits are all available from third parties for the price of a dinner. The Evo Pro added a factory 9-bar OPV and stainless internals with an anti-corrosion coating, which fixed the two things people used to mod on day one. At $499 with a 58 mm commercial portafilter, this is the cheapest entry into the real espresso ecosystem, where every accessory and upgrade you buy carries over.

highlights.title: The De'Longhi Rivelia is the right call for households that will never dial in a shot
highlights.body: There is an honest case for a superautomatic, and it is this: a machine that gets used every day at 8.2 out of 10 beats a machine that gets used twice a month at 9.5. The Rivelia's 18 programmed recipes and full-colour touchscreen mean anyone in the house can make their own drink without instruction, and the swappable bean hoppers let one person run decaf while another runs a light roast, switching in about ten seconds. The LatteCrema system produces genuinely good microfoam at three density settings. At around $1,500 you are paying for consistency and zero friction rather than peak flavour, and for a lot of households that is the correct trade.

highlights.title: The $150 machine is a legitimate strategy, not a compromise
highlights.body: I recommend the De'Longhi Stilosa to about a third of people who ask me what espresso machine to buy, and I mean it every time. It has a real 15-bar pump, a stainless steel boiler, and a steam wand, so it produces actual pressurized espresso and actual steamed milk. Live with it for three months. If you find yourself making two drinks a day and getting frustrated by the pressurized basket, you have learned something worth $150 and you now know exactly which $700 machine to buy. If the novelty fades by week six, you saved yourself $550 and a large chunk of counter space.

highlights.title: Flow control is the one prosumer feature that changes what is in the cup
highlights.body: Most upgrades between $1,500 and $3,000 buy marginal gains: slightly better temperature stability, slightly faster recovery, nicer steel. The Lelit Bianca V3's manual paddle is different because it hands you a variable the machine cannot decide for you. Being able to open slowly for a low-pressure pre-infusion, ramp to 9 bar, then taper down as the shot runs lets you pull light roasts that would channel and sour on any fixed-pressure machine. If you buy single-origin light roasts and you already own a good grinder, this is the feature that justifies the jump. If you drink medium roast with milk, it will sit unused and the Breville Dual Boiler is the smarter $1,300 saved.

zh-tw.commentary:
2026 年我把 Breville Barista Express Impress 放在第一，理由很單純，它解決了最多人放棄自己煮咖啡的那個環節，磨豆。老實說一萬五以下的機器只要粉量跟填壓對了，萃取都不會太差，問題是新手根本抓不到那個手感，卡在這裡就收攤了。Impress 幫你磨、幫你定量、粉多了兩克它會告訴你，扳一下桿子就給你 22 磅的壓力。我看過不少原本只喝外面咖啡的人，用這台一個禮拜就能穩定拉出可以喝的濃縮，這件事的價值遠比高階機那一點點溫度穩定度來得實在。

第二名 Breville Dual Boiler 是進階玩家裡最不傷荷包的選擇。真雙鍋爐加 PID 加可程式化預浸泡，五萬有找，比同規格義大利機便宜快三萬，而萃取品質的差距小到多數人喝不出來。

第三名 Gaggia Classic Evo Pro 拿的是十五年的長線分數。58mm 商用把手、三通電磁閥，加上從九零年代活到現在的零件供應鏈，這台一萬七買下去，很可能就是你這輩子最後一台咖啡機。之後再花五千裝 PID 套件，實力直接跟四倍價格的機器對打。

Lelit Bianca V3 跟 Rancilio Silvia Pro X 排在第五第六，是照拿到手第一天的體驗來排的。這兩台的萃取水準確實贏過上面所有機器，但它們要求你先準備一台萬元磨豆機、一個電子秤、一支布粉器，還有真的願意花時間學。用三年後的表現來排，它們是第一跟第二。

第十名的 De'Longhi Stilosa 我還是要留在榜上。四千出頭，它回答的是花三萬之前唯一該問的問題，你是真的每天早上想煮咖啡，還是只是嚮往那個畫面？

highlights.title: 磨豆機才是主角，Breville 最早想通這件事
highlights.body: 濃縮咖啡九成勝負在研磨均勻度跟粉量精準度。一台十萬的 Lelit 配一台兩千塊的砍豆機，煮出來會輸給內建錐刀的 Impress，這個差距大到沒有爭論空間。Impress 還多做了一件事，填壓前螢幕會顯示粉量刻度，告訴你該補粉還是刮掉一點。這個功能直接消滅新手最常見的翻車原因，粉量忽多忽少，今天狂噴明天完全不出水。反過來想，如果你本來就要另外買磨豆機，預算至少要抓一萬二起跳，這樣算下來全能機的帳完全不一樣。

highlights.title: 想用到 2041 年就買 Gaggia Classic Evo Pro
highlights.body: 這是榜上唯一有幾十年實績可查的機種。Gaggia 從 1991 年就在生產 Classic 這個平台，零件生態也反映了這件事，墊圈、電磁閥、鍋爐、洩壓閥彈簧、整組 PID 改裝套件，副廠都有貨而且一頓好料的價格就買得到。Evo Pro 這一版原廠直接調好 9 bar 洩壓閥、內部改用不鏽鋼件加防鏽鍍層，把以前大家買回家第一件事就要改的兩個地方修掉了。一萬七配 58mm 商用把手，這是進入真正義式咖啡生態系最便宜的門票，之後買的每一個配件跟升級品都能繼續用。

highlights.title: 全家人都不會調粉的家庭，De'Longhi Rivelia 才是正解
highlights.body: 全自動機有一個很誠實的理由，一台每天都拿出來用的 8.2 分機器，贏過一個月只開兩次的 9.5 分機器。Rivelia 的 18 種預設配方加全彩觸控，意思是家裡任何人不用教就能做自己那杯，可抽換豆槽讓爸媽喝低咖啡因、你自己喝淺焙，十秒就切換完畢。LatteCrema 奶泡系統三段綿密度打出來的奶泡是真的好用。四萬五買的是穩定跟零摩擦，風味天花板本來就不是它的賣點，對很多家庭來說這個取捨完全正確。

highlights.title: 四千塊的機器是一種策略，不是將就
highlights.body: 來問我該買哪台咖啡機的人裡面，大概三分之一我會推 De'Longhi Stilosa，而且每次都是認真的。它有真的 15 bar 幫浦、不鏽鋼鍋爐、蒸汽管，萃出來是有壓力的濃縮，打出來是真的奶泡。先用三個月看看，如果你發現自己一天煮兩杯而且開始嫌加壓濾杯不夠玩，這四千塊買到的資訊很值，你也很清楚接下來該花三萬買哪一台。如果第六週熱情就退了，你省下兩萬六，還有一大塊流理台空間。

highlights.title: 流量控制是高階機裡唯一真正改變杯中風味的功能
highlights.body: 五萬到十萬這個區間的升級，多半買到的是邊際效益，溫度再穩一點、回溫再快一點、鋼材再漂亮一點。Lelit Bianca V3 的手動撥桿不一樣，它把一個機器沒辦法替你決定的變數交回你手上。可以先低壓慢慢預浸泡，拉到 9 bar，萃取後段再降下來，這樣才拉得動那種在固定壓力機上必定通道效應然後死酸的淺焙豆。如果你都買單品淺焙而且磨豆機已經到位，這個功能值得你跨過去。如果你平常喝中焙加牛奶，這根桿子會放到生灰，省下那八萬買 Breville Dual Boiler 聰明得多。

references.title: 6 Best Espresso Machines of 2026, Tested and Reviewed

en.commentary: A quiet week in espresso, and I mean that as a compliment. The latest round of testing from Consumer Reports and Forbes lands on the same conclusion I keep reaching, that the best machines of 2026 are mostly the machines that were already best, with the improvements going into reliability and daily usability rather than new extraction tricks. That is exactly why my order holds. The Breville Barista Express Impress stays at number one because it does the single hardest thing for a beginner, it removes the mess and guesswork from dosing and tamping while still teaching real technique. With more than 27,000 reviews sitting around 4.5 stars, it is the machine I hand to anyone who wants cafe drinks without a workshop. The Breville Dual Boiler keeps second for people who care about the shot itself, since dual boilers give the temperature stability and steam-on-demand that milk drinks and back-to-back pulls reward. The Gaggia Classic Evo Pro holds third on pure build value, a metal machine you can repair for years at a price that keeps embarrassing pricier rivals. At the top of the automatic lane, the De'Longhi Rivelia earns its place with 18 programmed recipes that genuinely rival manual shots for hands-off households. And at the entry point, the De'Longhi Stilosa still makes real espresso with a steam wand for under 155 dollars, the cleanest way to find out if home espresso is your thing before spending more. No rank changes this week, because nothing on the market earned one.

highlights.title: Best-in-class stayed best
highlights.body: Fresh 2026 testing from Consumer Reports and Forbes confirms the top machines improved on reliability, not extraction. That is why every rank holds this week.

highlights.title: Barista Express Impress for beginners
highlights.body: It removes the mess from dosing and tamping while teaching real technique, and its 27,000-plus reviews near 4.5 stars back up the number one spot.

highlights.title: Dual Boiler for shot chasers
highlights.body: Dual boilers deliver the temperature stability and steam-on-demand that milk drinks and back-to-back pulls need, keeping it second for enthusiasts.

highlights.title: Stilosa is still the cheapest real start
highlights.body: Under 155 dollars with a steam wand, it makes genuine espresso and is the low-risk way to test whether home espresso suits you before upgrading.

zh-tw.commentary: 這週義式咖啡機很安靜，我當它是讚美。Consumer Reports 和 Forbes 最新實測都指向同一結論，2026 年最好的機器大多還是原本那幾台，改進放在耐用度和好不好用，所以我名次不動。Breville Barista Express Impress 穩坐第一，它幫新手把填粉壓粉的髒亂和猜測都拿掉，同時教你真手法，兩萬七千多則評價維持 4.5 星上下，想在家喝咖啡館水準又不想搞得像修車廠的人我直接推它。Breville Dual Boiler 守第二，雙鍋爐給溫度穩定和隨時打奶泡，連續出杯也不掉溫，適合在意那杯本身的人。Gaggia Classic Evo Pro 靠全金屬做工和可維修性排第三，價格一直讓貴很多的對手尷尬。全自動端 De'Longhi Rivelia 靠 18 種內建配方站穩，入門端 De'Longhi Stilosa 台幣約四千出頭就有蒸氣管、煮真正義式濃縮，是花大錢前先確認自己愛不愛玩咖啡最乾淨的方式。

highlights.title: 最強的還是最強
highlights.body: Consumer Reports 和 Forbes 的 2026 新測都確認頂端機器進步在耐用度而非萃取，所以這週名次全數維持。

highlights.title: 新手選 Barista Express Impress
highlights.body: 它把填粉壓粉的髒亂拿掉又教你真手法，兩萬七千多則評價近 4.5 星，第一名實至名歸。

highlights.title: 追萃取選 Dual Boiler
highlights.body: 雙鍋爐給溫度穩定和隨時打奶泡，連續出杯不掉溫，發燒友的第二名沒懸念。

highlights.title: Stilosa 仍是最便宜的真起點
highlights.body: 台幣約四千出頭有蒸氣管、煮真正義式濃縮，升級前先確認自己愛不愛在家玩，風險最低。

en.commentary: The Breville Barista Express Impress keeps my top overall spot, and it earns it by making great espresso approachable. The assisted Impress tamping system takes the guesswork out of the puck, the built-in grinder means one machine does the whole job, and PID temperature control keeps shots consistent day to day. For most people who want cafe-quality coffee without a learning cliff, this is the machine. The Breville Dual Boiler holds second as the enthusiast's Breville. Dual boilers let you pull and steam at once with rock-solid temperature stability, and its shot quality is a step above the Express. The Gaggia Classic Evo Pro stays third and it is the tinkerer's darling. A commercial-style 58mm portafilter, 9-bar extraction, and simple rugged internals make it the most upgradeable and repairable machine here, which is why enthusiasts keep it for a decade. The Bambino Plus holds fourth as the value pick, with fast heat-up and surprisingly good milk texturing in a tiny footprint. Higher up the ceiling, the Lelit Bianca V3 and Rancilio Silvia Pro X reward skill with the best shot quality and milk steaming on the list. This week brought no launches that reshuffle the order, so I am carrying it forward. The theme holds: Breville wins on ease, Gaggia and the Italians win on craft, and the choice comes down to whether you want to brew or to tinker.

highlights.title: Barista Express Impress makes it easy
highlights.body: Assisted tamping, a built-in grinder, and PID control make cafe-quality shots approachable. It is the pick for most people.

highlights.title: Dual Boiler is the enthusiast Breville
highlights.body: Simultaneous pull-and-steam with rock-solid temperature stability puts its shot quality a clear step above the Express.

highlights.title: Gaggia Classic Evo Pro is the tinkerer's darling
highlights.body: A 58mm commercial portafilter and rugged internals make it the most upgradeable and repairable machine on the list.

highlights.title: Bambino Plus is the value entry
highlights.body: Fast heat-up and surprisingly good milk texturing in a tiny footprint keep it firmly in the value spot.

zh-tw.commentary: Breville Barista Express Impress 續坐我的綜合第一，它靠把好咖啡變得親民贏得這個位置。輔助式 Impress 填壓系統把粉餅的猜測拿掉，內建磨豆機讓一台機器搞定整套流程，PID 控溫讓每天的萃取都穩定。對多數想在家喝到咖啡館水準又不想面對陡峭學習曲線的人來說，就是這台。Breville 雙鍋爐守第二，是玩家版的 Breville。雙鍋爐讓你同時萃取跟打奶泡，溫度穩如磐石，萃取品質比 Express 高一階。Gaggia Classic Evo Pro 守第三，是愛折騰玩家的心頭好。商用規格的 58mm 沖煮把手、9 bar 萃取、簡單耐操的內部結構，讓它成為這裡最好升級也最好維修的機器，所以玩家會留著它用十年。Bambino Plus 守第四，是性價比選擇，加熱快，奶泡打得意外地好，體積還小巧。往天花板走，Lelit Bianca V3 跟 Rancilio Silvia Pro X 用最強的萃取品質跟蒸氣打奶回報你的技術。這週沒有新機攪動排名，照舊延續。主旋律不變：Breville 贏在好上手，Gaggia 跟義大利機贏在工藝，怎麼選就看你想沖咖啡還是想折騰。

highlights.title: Barista Express Impress 讓一切變簡單
highlights.body: 輔助填壓、內建磨豆機、PID 控溫，把咖啡館水準變親民。多數人的首選。

highlights.title: 雙鍋爐是玩家版 Breville
highlights.body: 同時萃取打奶又溫度穩如磐石，萃取品質明顯比 Express 高一階。

highlights.title: Gaggia Classic Evo Pro 是折騰玩家的最愛
highlights.body: 58mm 商用把手加耐操結構，是榜單裡最好升級也最好維修的機器。

highlights.title: Bambino Plus 是性價比入門
highlights.body: 加熱快、奶泡打得意外地好，體積又小巧，穩坐性價比位置。

references.title: Best Espresso Machines of 2026: 5 Proven Picks

en.commentary: The Barista Express Impress stays at the top because it does the one thing most home setups get wrong: it takes the guesswork out of dosing and tamping. The assisted Impress puck system gives you a consistent, level tamp every pull, and the built-in grinder means you go from beans to shot on one counter footprint. For most people learning espresso this is the machine that actually gets used every morning, and that is worth more than a spec sheet. The Dual Boiler holds second on pure shot quality, with temperature stability and steam power that outclass everything below it, and reviewers this month still rate it the choice for anyone who wants cafe results and does not mind the learning curve. The story of the week is the Gaggia Classic Evo Pro, which keeps drawing praise as the machine that rewards effort more than anything near its roughly $450 price. Testers keep noting that diehards add a PID for temperature stability and then keep it for years, and that repairability is why it stays a cult favorite. It holds third on unbeatable value and build. The Bambino Plus remains the small-kitchen pick, heating in seconds and steaming milk well above its price class. The rest of the order is unchanged because nothing this week shifted the fundamentals, and these machines earn their spots on shot quality, milk texture, and how likely you are to keep using them.

highlights.title: Barista Express Impress removes the guesswork
highlights.body: The assisted Impress tamping system delivers a level, consistent puck every pull, and the built-in grinder keeps everything on one footprint. It is the machine beginners actually keep using, which is why it leads.

highlights.title: Dual Boiler owns shot quality
highlights.body: Temperature stability and steam power set the Breville Dual Boiler apart for anyone chasing cafe-grade results at home. Reviewers this month still call it the top pick once you are past the basics.

highlights.title: Gaggia Classic Evo Pro is the value cult favorite
highlights.body: At around $450 it rewards effort more than any machine near its price, and its repairability keeps owners upgrading rather than replacing. A PID mod turns it into a lifelong shot puller.

highlights.title: Bambino Plus fits the small kitchen
highlights.body: Heats in seconds and textures milk well above its price class. For tight counters that still want real espresso and a proper microfoam, it stays the compact pick.

highlights.title: The order held on fundamentals
highlights.body: No release this week moved the needle, so ranks stay put. These machines earn their spots on shot quality, milk texture, build, and how often they get used day to day.

zh-tw.commentary: Barista Express Impress 繼續坐第一，因為它把大多數家用機最容易搞砸的一件事解決掉了：粉量跟壓粉的猜測。輔助式 Impress 壓粉系統每次都給你平整一致的粉餅，內建磨豆機讓你從咖啡豆到一杯濃縮只佔一個檯面。對大多數在學濃縮的人來說，這台才是每天早上真的會拿來用的機器，這比規格表上的數字值錢多了。Dual Boiler 守第二，純粹靠萃取品質，溫度穩定度跟蒸氣力道都贏過下面所有對手，這個月的評測還是把它列為想要咖啡館等級、又不介意學習曲線的人的首選。這週的重點是 Gaggia Classic Evo Pro，一直被誇是在它大約台幣一萬四價位帶裡，最能回報你下功夫的機器。測試的人一直提到，死忠玩家會加裝 PID 做溫度控制，然後一用就是好幾年，而且好維修就是它一直是邪教級愛機的原因。它靠無敵的性價比跟做工守住第三。Bambino Plus 還是小廚房的首選，幾秒就熱好，打奶泡的水準遠超它的價格。其餘排名沒動，因為這週沒有任何事情動搖基本盤，這些機器靠的是萃取品質、奶泡質地，還有你到底會不會一直用它。

highlights.title: Barista Express Impress 幫你把猜測拿掉
highlights.body: 輔助式 Impress 壓粉系統每次都給你平整一致的粉餅，內建磨豆機讓一切只佔一個檯面。這是新手真的會一直用的機器，所以排第一。

highlights.title: Dual Boiler 拿下萃取品質王座
highlights.body: 溫度穩定度跟蒸氣力道，讓 Breville Dual Boiler 在追求家用咖啡館等級的人裡脫穎而出。這個月的評測還是說，過了入門階段它就是首選。

highlights.title: Gaggia Classic Evo Pro 是性價比邪教機
highlights.body: 大約台幣一萬四，卻比同價位任何機器都更會回報你下的功夫，好維修讓人選擇升級而不是換機。加個 PID 就變成能陪你一輩子的萃取機。

highlights.title: Bambino Plus 最適合小廚房
highlights.body: 幾秒就熱好，打奶泡的水準遠超它的價位。檯面很擠又想要真正濃縮跟綿密奶泡的人，它還是那台小體積首選。

highlights.title: 排名靠基本盤守住
highlights.body: 這週沒有新品動搖大局，所以排名不動。這些機器靠的是萃取品質、奶泡質地、做工，還有你每天到底會不會拿出來用。
